name: Mondéjar and Vía Verde del Tajuña Loop
name_original: Mondejar a Orusco de Tajuña a Ambite — circular
description: This moderate 24.9-mile mountain bike loop on the Vía Verde del Tajuña offers diverse terrain and historical sights near Mondéjar.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Mondéjar traverse a varied landscape characterized by rolling hills and river valleys within the La Alcarria region. The area features extensive agricultural lands, including vineyards and olive groves, alongside serene pine forests. Terrain ranges from mostly paved surfaces to unpaved and technical sections, offering diverse riding experiences. Best mountain bike trails in Mondéjar The most popular mountain bike trail is Ambite a Estremera a Mondéjar — circular por la Vía Verde del Tajuña, a 34.3 miles (55.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 7 minutes to complete. This route follows the historic Tajuña Greenway, offering scenic views along the river valley. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Mondéjar and Tajuña Greenway Loop, a moderate 15.7 miles (25.2 km) path. This loop integrates sections of the Tajuña Greenway with surrounding rural landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Tajuña Greenway and 40-Day Train Trail Loop, a 13.7 miles (22.0 km) trail leading through the Tajuña river valley and along an old railway line, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Mountain biking around Mondéjar is defined by rolling hills, river valleys, and agricultural lands, with routes that cater to various skill levels from easy to difficult. The network includes sections of the Vía Verde del Tajuña and diverse natural terrain. The routes in Mondéjar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Mondéjar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Mondéjar?
A: There are over 90 mountain bike trails around Mondéjar, offering a diverse range of experiences for all skill levels. The region is well-known for its varied topography, from rolling hills to river valleys.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Mondéjar?
A: Yes, Mondéjar offers 12 easy mountain bike trails, perfect for beginners or family outings. These routes often feature gentler terrain, such as sections of the historic Vía Verde del Tajuña. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1317079090/tajuna-greenway-and-40-day-train-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway and 40-Day Train Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.7 miles (22.0 km) long and leads through the scenic Tajuña river valley.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Mondéjar?
A: The terrain around Mondéjar is quite varied. You'll encounter rolling hills, picturesque river valleys, and extensive agricultural lands with vineyards and olive groves. Some routes, like those along the Vía Verde del Tajuña, also pass through serene pine forests. Expect a mix of mostly paved surfaces, unpaved paths, and some technical sections, catering to different riding preferences.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Mondéjar?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Mondéjar features 5 difficult mountain bike trails. These routes often include steep sections, rocks, and roots, providing a more technical and demanding ride. The region is part of the 'Circuito MTB Guadalajara,' which hosts events and confirms its standing as a destination for serious mountain biking.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Mondéjar?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Mondéjar are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005604641/mondejar-and-tajuna-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondéjar and Tajuña Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15.7 miles (25.2 km) path that integrates sections of the Tajuña Greenway with surrounding rural landscapes, offering a convenient circular ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Mondéjar?
A: The mountain bike trails in Mondéjar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views along the Tajuña Greenway, and the opportunity to explore the unique rural landscapes of the La Alcarria region.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: While riding, you can encounter several interesting points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3501590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmores Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, an Asset of Heritage Interest, is linked to historical routes. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3825118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Junction of the Tajuña Greenway and the Tren de los 40 Días Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, a significant point for cyclists. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Goyeneche Palace of Nuevo Baztán&lt;/a&gt; is a cultural highlight nearby.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique natural features to explore?
A: Yes, the area boasts several natural monuments. You can find the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3255898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encina de la Pica&lt;/a&gt; (Holm Oak) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7267570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas Blancas Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;. These natural highlights offer beautiful spots to pause and appreciate the regional landscape.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Mondéjar?
A: One of the longest routes is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314901394/pastrana-a-recopolis-a-mondejar-circular-desde-ambite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pastrana a Recópolis a Mondejar — circular desde Ambite&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 52.1 miles (83.8 km) trail. This route offers an extensive ride through the diverse landscapes of the region, including significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that connect different villages in the Alcarria region?
A: Yes, some routes are designed to explore the charming villages of the Alcarria region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316382592/alcarria-villages-loop-from-ambite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcarria Villages Loop from Ambite&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 29.8 miles (48.0 km) trail that allows you to discover the rural character and scenic beauty between different settlements.

Q: Is the Vía Verde del Tajuña suitable for mountain biking?
A: The Vía Verde del Tajuña is an excellent feature for mountain biking in the area. This historic greenway, an old railway line, offers accessible and scenic routes. While generally considered easy, some gravel sections can present a moderate challenge, requiring good physical fitness.
tips: Renowned for its wines with denomination of origin, this town in the interior of Guadalajara has more to offer and is worth visiting. Among the highlights are the ruins of the Convent of San Antonio, one of the first in the Renaissance style in Spain, its Plaza Mayor and the Church of Santa María Magdalena.
This dirt track runs through the typical countryside landscape of the Las Vegas region, planted with cereals, olive trees and vineyards.
Palacio de Ambite was built in 1623 by the Marquis of Legarda.
Here you will find a fantastic gravel road perfect for mountain biking or gravel biking. This road runs alongside the Tajuña River between Ambite and the Highway to Olmeda.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Mondéjar and Vía Verde del Tajuña Loop for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as moderate for mountain biking. While sections of the Vía Verde del Tajuña are generally easy, this specific loop requires good physical condition and some prior cycling experience due to its length and varied terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite diverse. You'll ride on sections of the Vía Verde del Tajuña, which features reddish asphalt in some parts, but also compacted earth and gravel, especially past Ambite and within the municipality of Mondéjar. Expect gravel paths through cultivated fields and gentle inclines, with original railway infrastructure like bridges and viaducts.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Mondéjar and Vía Verde del Tajuña Loop?
faqs.answer: Mondéjar serves as an excellent starting point for this loop. You can find parking within the town. Alternatively, the Vía Verde del Tajuña itself starts in Arganda del Rey, which is accessible via Madrid's metro system, offering another option if you plan to connect to the loop from there.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Mondéjar and Vía Verde del Tajuña Loop?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on Vías Verdes, but they should be kept on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or near agricultural land. Always ensure your dog is under control and clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are typically the best seasons for mountain biking this route. The weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for a more comfortable ride through the varied landscapes.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the Vía Verde del Tajuña or this loop. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What are some notable sights or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting features. Look out for the historic railway infrastructure, including old train stations, iron bridges, and viaducts. Near Mondéjar, there's a remarkable 96-meter-long curved stone bridge. The route also offers views of the Mondéjar settlement, the Brea de Tajo to Mondéjar Dirt Road, and a View of Palacio de Ambite.

faqs.question: Does this loop connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll ride along parts of the Ruta Verde 3.3 Vía Verde del Tajuña, the Vía Verde del Tajuña itself, the Camino de Brea a Mondéjar, and the Camino de Fuentenovilla. It also briefly touches the Cañada Real Soriana Oriental.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while riding?
faqs.answer: The loop offers diverse scenery, including pleasant stretches alongside the Tajuña River, fertile lowlands (vegas), areas of scrubland, and dry farming fields. You'll also pass through pine forests, orchards, and other agricultural landscapes, characteristic of the La Alcarria region, known as 'the land of honey'.

faqs.question: Is this loop suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: While the Vía Verde del Tajuña itself is generally considered easy and suitable for families, this specific 40 km loop is rated as 'moderate' for mountain biking. It requires a good physical condition and some prior cycling experience, making it more suitable for families with older, more experienced riders rather than young children or beginners.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or drink near the trail?
faqs.answer: Mondéjar, being a settlement and a good starting point, will have options for cafes and restaurants. As you pass through other towns like Ambite, you may also find local establishments to refuel.
